# dumbometer

> A Claude Code status-line gauge that shows how full your context window is, from Smart to Dumb, so you /compact before your AI's quality drops. Free, MIT, zero tokens, never crashes.

dumbometer is a Claude Code plugin. It reads the context-window percentage Claude Code already computes and renders a 14-cell, 256-color gauge in your status line: Smart (0-24%), Coasting (25-49%), Foggy (50-69%), Cooked (70-89%), Dumb (90-100%). It runs locally on every render, costs zero tokens, has zero dependencies, and is built to never crash the status line.

## Install
- /plugin marketplace add MaximoCorrea1/dumbometer
- /plugin install dumbometer
- /dumbometer:setup

## Key facts
- Free and MIT licensed. The free tier is the whole product.
- Zero token cost: reads a number Claude Code already computed, never calls the model.
- Zero dependencies, Node.js only, 64 tests, sub-millisecond hot path.
- Works with both the 200k and 1M context windows.
- "Context rot" is the quality decay that happens as the context window fills. dumbometer measures the cause (context percent) so you can see it coming.

## Pages
- Home: https://dumbometer.xyz
- Pricing (machine readable): https://dumbometer.xyz/pricing.md
- Source (free): https://github.com/MaximoCorrea1/dumbometer

## Pricing
- Free: $0. The complete product.
- Big Dumb Energy: $9 one-time. Adds color themes, Roast Mode, custom labels, a dumb score flag.
- Singularity: $1,000,000 per year. A joke. Please buy the free one.
